title: US5: AI triage application (demo) — auto-trigger on ticket.created + Mode-1 triage + gated draft_reply → approve → reply
description: Spec 003 US5 (the acceptance demo). Builds on l29's async event-driven run trigger. An enabled agent auto-runs on ticket.created (loop-guarded: subscribe ONLY to ticket.created, never message.received, so an agent reply can't re-trigger). Mode 1 auto-applies reversible triage (status/priority/tags/assignee) and queues the External draft_reply; a human approves in the US4 queue and the reply is sent via the existing ticketing.Reply outbox. Architecture: (1) ticket.created subscriber creates a queued agent_run per enabled agent, idempotent on the triggering message_id; (2) a run-drain poller claims queued runs (queued->running state-claim, SKIP LOCKED, system-wide definer fn) and calls Engine.Execute. Reuses the US4 approve->outbox->idempotent-executor pattern symmetrically. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-us5-triage-demo.md
close_reason: Closed

title: US4: Autonomy gate + approvals queue (fail-closed gate, approval_item, 3-mode matrix, idempotent outbox execute, agents.approve)
description: Spec 003 US4 (design §5, §3.3). Replace runner.go execTool's degenerate 'non-Safe⇒proposed' rule with the real autonomy gate: classify tool EffectClass (split Safe into Read+Reversible) × agent AutonomyMode → auto-exec | needs-approval, fail-closed on unknown. Add approval_item RLS table (mig 0030), agents.approve permission (mig 0031, NOT granted to agent_runtime — agents must not self-approve), approvals-queue HTTP (list/approve/deny) + OpenAPI/drift, and idempotent approval execution via the existing transactional outbox (approve→outbox event→ApprovalExecutor subscriber executes ticketing.Reply/Triage as the agent, exactly-once via approval-id dedup key). Expire sweep for stale pending items. Security-regression pins per design §4 (gate fail-closed, gate-after-RBAC ordering, mode matrix, idempotent replay, approval_item RLS, agents.approve not on agent_runtime). Parent epic manyforge-deo.
close_reason: US4 shipped & full gate green (test/contract/lint/sec/int). Fail-closed autonomy gate (EffectClass split Read/Reversible × 3-mode matrix) replaces US3 propose-only seam; approval_item RLS table (0030); agents.approve human-only perm (0031) + DB membership-guard enforcement (0033); approvals-queue HTTP (list/approve/deny) + OpenAPI/drift; transactional approve→outbox→idempotent ApprovalExecutor (state-claim + reply dedup key, exactly-once proven by replay integration test); system-wide expire sweep (0032 SECURITY DEFINER). 5 new US4 security pins + guard-safe extension. Commits e312b9a..6364d2c. Final holistic review: READY TO MERGE. Follow-ups filed for approval_item.error/'failed' state.

title: US3: Agent run loop + internal tool registry + audit + cost + budget guard
description:
Spec 003 US3 (design §5). Build the bounded agentic run loop that drives an agent definition (US2) through the AI gateway (US1) to act on spec-002 tickets.

Scope:
- agent_run table (RLS, migration 0028; design §3.2): id, agent_id, business_id, tenant_root_id, trigger, target_type/target_id, status(queued/running/awaiting_approval/succeeded/failed), tokens_in/out, cost_cents, correlation_id, error, timestamps.
- Internal tool registry wrapping ticketing.Service (read_ticket, read_thread, set_status, set_priority, set_tags, set_assignee, draft_reply) — each carries a JSON-schema + static effect-class (Safe/External/Irreversible); typed-arg validation so LLM output never reaches SQL/shell (Principle IV).
- Bounded loop: max_iterations + max_tokens-per-run + wall-clock (config; defaults decided in plan); per-turn provider call via ai.Provider.Complete; tool-call→tool-result re-entry.
- Budget-cap run-start guard: refuse to start when month-to-date cost >= agent.monthly_budget_cents (status=failed, audited).
- Per-run audit via existing audit_entry (actor=agent principal, correlation_id threads the run).
- Token/cost capture via ai.Registry.Lookup(model).CostCents(usage).

Resolves deferred items:
- manyforge-deo §3.5: bind the agent principal's membership + a guard-safe role granting agents.run (NEW permission to seed) + tickets.{read,reply,write,assign}, so the agent passes RLS/RBAC to act.
- manyforge-1oq: agent-deletion FK semantics once an agent has acted (map 23503->409 / disable).
- manyforge-4po: AI Request temperature/sampling default.

US3 ships a degenerate fail-closed execution seam (Safe tools execute inline; non-Safe are proposed-only + audited, run goes awaiting_approval). US4 replaces that seam with the real autonomy gate + approval_item queue + three-mode matrix.

Regression contract (US3-owned pins): deterministic runs via ai.MockProvider/golden fixtures; every proposed+executed action audited; runs tenant-isolated (RLS, no-oracle 404); agent cannot invoke a tool outside allowed_tools; typed-arg validation pin; budget cap refuses start.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-us3-run-loop.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: US2: agent definitions CRUD (business-bound agent table + AgentService over RLS)
description:
Spec 003 US2 (design §5). Add a business-bound 'agent' table and an AgentService doing CRUD over the RLS DB, with the agents.configure permission, ownership predicates, and no-oracle 404s.

Scope (design §3.2 columns): agent{ id, business_id, tenant_root_id, principal_id (→ kind='agent' principal), provider, model, system_prompt, allowed_tools text[], autonomy_mode smallint (1/2/3 default 1), enabled bool, monthly_budget_cents int, timestamps }. CreateAgent creates/links a kind='agent' principal scoped to the business. AgentService.{Create,Get,List,Update,Delete} run under db.WithPrincipal (RLS), dual-enforced ownership predicate (WHERE business scoping in SQL), foreign-UUID and unknown-UUID return the SAME not-found (no 403/404 oracle). New RLS table mirrors migration 0025 exactly (ENABLE not FORCE, authorized_businesses(current_principal()) policy, GRANT to manyforge_app, full reversible .down.sql) + db/schema.sql entry + db/query + sqlc generate. Add agents.configure to the RBAC permission catalog (migration after 0025) + role presets. Map errs via the credential.go mapErr idiom (pgx.ErrNoRows→ErrNotFound, 23505→ErrConflict).

Builds on US1a credential.go + migration 0025; mirrors internal/ticketing service/RLS patterns. Test plan: unit (validation) + integration (CRUD + seal-free round-trip + cross-tenant RLS no-oracle isolation via testdb/seedAgentTenant) + security_regression pins (tenant isolation, ownership predicate). HTTP/OpenAPI surface scope TBD at plan time (US1a creds were service-layer-only).
close_reason: Closed

title: US1b: live AI provider transports (Anthropic + OpenAI-compat) behind ai.Provider
description:
Implement the two live provider transports that satisfy the ai.Provider interface defined in US1a (manyforge-79s), plus golden-fixture replay test machinery.

Scope:
- internal/platform/ai/anthropic.go — Anthropic Messages API; map common schema <-> Messages request/response incl tool_use content blocks, usage tokens, stop_reason.
- internal/platform/ai/openaicompat.go — OpenAI-compatible chat/completions incl tool_calls; user-supplied base_url covers OpenAI/Ollama/vLLM. MUST build the HTTP client via netsafe.NewClient (SSRF guard) for the user-supplied base_url. HTTPS where applicable.
- Hand-rolled over net/http (NO vendor SDK — confirmed absent from go.mod).
- Recorded golden-fixture machinery: record a real response once, replay in CI (extends the US1a MockProvider idea); no live API calls in CI.
- Wire both into the ai.Registry so credential.go provider resolution can construct them by provider name.

Test plan: unit tests with httptest server + golden fixtures for request marshaling and response parsing (happy path, tool_use/tool_calls, usage tokens, error bodies -> typed errors, SSRF rejection of RFC1918/metadata base_url). 

Builds on US1a: internal/platform/ai/{schema,provider,registry,mock}.go and internal/agents/credential.go. Part of epic Spec 003 Agent Runtime.
close_reason: Closed

title: T039 send subscriber blocked: principal-less worker can't read/write RLS send tables
description:
The outbound send subscriber (internal/platform/notify/sender_subscriber.go) and the bounce path both call plain dbgen queries against RLS-protected tables (ticket_message, inbound_address) from the principal-less outbox worker (manyforge_app, NOBYPASSRLS, current_principal()=NULL).

Empirically (integration probe): authorized_businesses(NULL) returns 0 rows, so the ticket_message/inbound_address RLS USING predicate matches 0 rows. GetMessageDeliveryState/GetBusinessSystemInboundAddress return pgx.ErrNoRows and MarkMessageDelivered/MarkMessageFailed UPDATE 0 rows (silent no-op) when run via DB.WithTx.

Affected dbgen queries (all plain-table, all driven by the principal-less worker per their own doc comments): GetMessageDeliveryState, GetBusinessSystemInboundAddress, MarkMessageDelivered, MarkMessageFailed, GetOutboundMessageForBounce.

Fix (in-pattern, mirrors claim_outbox_batch / ingest_inbound_message in migrations 0014/0016): add a new migration (0019_*) with SECURITY DEFINER functions for the send/bounce delivery-state path, GRANT EXECUTE to manyforge_app, and repoint these db/query/ticketing.sql entries at them (RETURNS-TABLE DEFINERs via raw pgx per the plan's tech-stack note), then make generate. Out of scope for the T039 subscriber task (notify/main/config only); the plan's T035-prep landed these as plain-table queries and asserted 'no DEFINER change', which is the root oversight.

The subscriber + main.go wiring + config + DKIM are otherwise implemented and compile; only the DB layer below blocks the integration test (TestSendHappyPath/Suppressed/Idempotent).
close_reason: Fixed via migration 0019 SECURITY DEFINER (get_send_context + mark_message_delivery); subscriber repointed to raw-pgx DEFINER calls; send integration tests green.

title: US1 reply-token doesn't encode ticket's own id — p_hint_ticket threading fallback is dead
description:
In ingest_inbound_message (migrations/0014), the hint path resolves WHERE id = p_hint_ticket. But Go's inbox.Ingest mints reply_token over a FRESH uuid (not the ticket's row id, which the DB default generates), only to satisfy reply_token NOT NULL. So a later inbound reply carrying that token decodes to a uuid that never equals any ticket.id → the reply-token/VERP threading FALLBACK never matches. US1 threading is unaffected (it uses In-Reply-To/References header matching, which works + is tested). This breaks US2 outbound→inbound reply-token threading.

FIX (do in US2, TDD): write a failing test that (1) creates a ticket, (2) stamps Reply-To with SignReplyToken(ticket.id,key), (3) sends an inbound reply carrying that token with NO matching In-Reply-To header, (4) asserts it threads to the same ticket. It will fail. Then fix via the cleanest option: add p_ticket_id arg to ingest_inbound_message (new forward-only migration 0017, CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION) so Go generates the ticket uuid v7, signs the token over it, and passes both — making the stored ticket.reply_token coherent with the row id. (Alt: two-step UPDATE of reply_token after insert in the same tx.)

Minor related: attachment blob Key currently uses (tenantRoot, business, attachmentID, attachmentID) since ticket id isn't known pre-insert; functionally correct + tenant-scoped + unique, but a later task may want the real ticket id embedded (would require storing attachments after the ingest call). Flagged f2339ec.
notes:
RESOLVED. TDD surfaced TWO conspiring root causes, both fixed:
(1) id-coherence (documented): inbox.Ingest signed reply_token over uuid.New() (≠ ticket.id). Fix: migration 0017 adds p_ticket_id to ingest_inbound_message; Go now mints the ticket id (uuid v7), signs the token over it, and passes it as the new ticket's id.
(2) case-corruption (NEW, found via end-to-end RED): normalizeRecipient lowercased the WHOLE recipient incl. the case-sensitive base64url plus-token → VerifyReplyToken always failed. Fix: lowercase only the routing key (local+domain), preserve plus-token case verbatim.
Tests: internal/inbox/resolve_test.go (unit, case-preservation) + TestIngestRepliesThreadViaReplyToken (integration, end-to-end thread). Full gate green; migration 0017 up/down/up verified reversible.
close_reason: Reply-token/VERP threading fallback now works (two root causes fixed: id-coherence via migration 0017 p_ticket_id + plus-token case-preservation in normalizeRecipient). TDD red→green, full gate + migration reversibility verified.

At run start, connect (netsafe SSRF guard, loopback-dev flag) → tools/list → register tools namespaced mcp:<server>:<tool>, Effect=External, RequiredPerm=mcp.invoke (new perm, granted agent_runtime). All MCP tools fail-closed → approval → executor reconnects → tools/call (at-least-once, idempotency hint). Remote Streamable-HTTP only; stdio + per-tool reclassification deferred. title: Hardening: fail-fast on DB schema drift (server refuses to serve a migration behind the code)
description: Today a dev DB stuck at migration 24 against branch-003 code caused opaque 500s ('We couldn't load this conversation') because ticket_message queries select source_approval_item_id (mig 0030) which didn't exist. The server boots + /readyz passes even when the schema is behind the code, then 500s at query time. Fix: at startup (and/or /readyz) compare applied schema_migrations.version against the code's expected latest migration; fail loudly ('DB schema behind: applied N, expected M; run manyforge migrate') instead of serving. Source: spec-003 session 2026-06-04.
close_reason: Closed

title: US5 prerequisite: async event-driven agent run trigger (outbox worker)
description: US3 ships synchronous Engine.Run + a manual HTTP trigger (POST /businesses/{id}/agents/{agentID}/runs, gated agents.run). Design §3.3 wants event-driven enqueue via the outbox + a worker draining queued agent_run rows as the agent principal. Build for the US5 triage demo; reuse spec-002's is_auto_reply loop-guard so agent-authored replies never re-trigger triage. The agent_run.trigger column already supports 'event'.
close_reason: Closed

title: US3: agent deletion semantics once an agent has acted (FK RESTRICT on author_principal_id/notification)
description:
US2's DeleteAgent does a hard DELETE FROM principal (CTE) for the agent's kind='agent' principal. In US2 this is clean (no agent has acted). But FK review (US2 final review) found: ticket_message.author_principal_id (migration 0013) and notification.principal_id (0016) reference principal with NO ON DELETE action (RESTRICT). Once a US3+ agent authors a ticket_message (e.g. an approved reply) or receives a notification, DeleteAgent's principal delete will FK-violate (SQLSTATE 23503) — and mapAgentErr has no 23503 case, so it surfaces as a 500.

US3/US5 must decide agent-deletion semantics for agents that have acted: either (a) soft-delete/disable instead of hard delete (set enabled=false, keep the principal + audit trail), or (b) ON DELETE SET NULL on author_principal_id (keep the message, null the author), or (c) map 23503 → ErrConflict (409 'agent has activity, disable instead'). Option (a)+(c) preferred (preserves audit per Principle VI). Surfaced by US2 final review; not a US2 bug (no agent acts in US2).
close_reason: Fixed in 2438d3a: mapAgentErr maps 23503 FK-violation on agent delete to 409 (disable-instead); never-acted agents still cascade-delete cleanly. Pinned by agent_delete_fk_pin_test.go.

title: List assignable members endpoint for ticket assignee picker

Frontend currently supports unassign + assign-to-self/manual-uuid. Add GET /businesses/{id}/assignable-members (or similar) returning eligible member principals, then enrich the thread-view assignee control into a real picker. Relates to US3/epic manyforge-n0q.
close_reason: Closed

title: SC-011: bounded mail-loop suppression + automated loop test
description: is_auto_reply is detected and stored on inbound messages but never ACTED upon to bound amplification. Acceptable for US2 (outbound is purely agent-initiated, no auto-send loop), but SC-011 requires a mail loop between two automated systems to be detected+suppressed before exceeding a bounded number of tickets/replies, verified by an automated loop test. Implement the bounded suppression + the loop test (Polish phase). Surfaced in US2 final review.
close_reason: Closed

title: US2 test-hardening: pin the ticket.replied producer↔consumer payload seam
description: The #1 integration seam (Reply enqueues a map; SendSubscriber decodes repliedPayload by json tag) is correct today but UNPINNED: no test drives the REAL enqueued event through the subscriber. reply_integration_test only asserts an outbox row exists; send_integration_test builds its own payload map. A future producer-key rename would silently decode to zero values -> get_send_context no-rows -> message dropped as terminal no-op (warn only), no test failure. Add an end-to-end test (call Reply -> read the actual outbox payload -> SendSubscriber.Handle -> assert the Mail) OR a source-level key-set pin, per the project's 'pin it so CI fails loud' rule. Surfaced in US2 final review.
close_reason: Closed

title: US2: reply/note missing new→open status transition (data-model L438)
description: data-model.md L438 specifies that an internal note and an outbound reply both set a ticket new→open if still 'new' (never close/solve implicitly). Neither ticketing.Service.Reply (T037) nor AddNote (T038) implements this transition + its in-tx audit. Surfaced during US2 Task 4 code review. Decide whether to fold into a US2 follow-up or US3 (triage/status). Add the new→open status update (with audit) to both write paths, in the same tx. NOTE: do NOT bump last_message_at on a note (that would resurface an internal note in the customer-activity sort — omission is correct per review).
close_reason: Implemented in T047: Reply and AddNote advance new→open in-tx with pinned ticket.status_changed audit (old_value plumbing). Covered by TestReplyAndNoteAdvanceNewToOpen.

title: Ticket list assembleTicket N+1 — optimize before SC-010 perf gate
description: T031 read slice: assembleTicket issues 3 extra round-trips per ticket (requester + tags + message_count), so a 100-row ticket-list page = 300+ queries. Correct + business-scoped + no security issue, but will likely blow the SC-010 ticket-list p95 budget under load. Optimize via JOIN/lateral/aggregate (single query assembling tags via array_agg, message_count via subquery/denormalized counter, requester via JOIN) when doing US1 Polish SC-010 perf work. Ref f10d4a8 internal/ticketing/service.go assembleTicket. Also (optional, minor): add a direct RequirePermission httptest asserting 404 body for a member lacking tickets.read.
close_reason: Closed

title: US5 polish T084: full green gate (make test + int-test + lint)

Spec: specs/001-tenant-foundation/spec.md (31 FRs, 11 SCs). Plan-level decisions to resolve in /speckit-plan: specs/001-tenant-foundation/plan-inputs.md.
close_reason: Tenant Foundation (spec 001) complete: US1-US5 (T001-T078) + polish T079-T085. Full gate green; all children closed.

title: US5 follow-up: optional re-triage on message.received (customer reply) with its own loop-guard
description: US5 deliberately triggers only on ticket.created (the structural loop-guard). Re-triaging on a customer's later reply (message.received) needs a dedicated guard so an agent-provoked inbound never re-triggers. Scope + design separately. Also a related wart: a queued run whose agent is deleted before drain stays queued (the claim JOINs agent, so it's never claimed) — decide expire/cleanup with manyforge-sa8 (terminal failed state).

title: US4 follow-up: surface approval_item failure reason + terminal 'failed' state
description: The approval_item.error column (mig 0030) and ApprovalItem.Error domain field are read but never written — the ApprovalExecutor records tool-invoke failures + unknown-tool to the audit log only. A dead-lettered approved item (outbox MaxAttempts exhausted) stays 'approved' forever with no operator-visible failure reason or terminal 'failed' state. Either: (a) populate error + add a 'failed' state the executor sets on permanent failure (operator visibility), or (b) drop the unused error column. Surfaced by US4 final holistic review. Non-blocking.

title: US3 follow-up: promote agent RunLimits + temperature to config keys
description: Loop bounds (max_iterations=8 / max_tokens_per_run=100k / max_output_tokens=4096 / wall_clock=120s) + temperature=0.0 ship as code defaults in internal/agents/runner.go (RunLimits + defaultTemperature), injected into the Engine in cmd/manyforge/main.go via RunLimits{}. Design §8 wants config keys. Wire them through the project config system and pass into the Engine at construction. Also consider hard concurrent-budget enforcement here (US3's mid-run guard reads month-to-date once and is best-effort/soft under concurrent runs — documented in runner.go).

title: US3: decide AI Request sampling-default handling (temperature unset vs explicit-0)
description:
US1b transports serialize Request.Temperature as-is. Because ai.Request.Temperature is a non-pointer float64, the transport cannot distinguish 'unset' from 'explicit 0', so an unset request sends temperature:0 (deterministic) to both Anthropic and OpenAI rather than the provider's natural default (~1.0). For a tool-calling agent runtime, deterministic-by-default is defensible, but it's currently implicit.

US3 decision when wiring agent definitions: either (a) make ai.Request.Temperature a *float64 (pointer; nil=provider default) — a US1a schema change rippling through both transports + mock, or (b) add ',omitempty' to the wire Temperature tags (unset 0 dropped → provider default; loses ability to force 0), or (c) keep current behavior and have agent definitions always set Temperature explicitly. Recommended: agent definition carries an explicit temperature (c), revisit pointer (a) only if model-default sampling is needed.

Surfaced by US1b final review. Files: internal/platform/ai/anthropic.go, openaicompat.go (Temperature wire fields), schema.go (Request.Temperature).
close_reason: Closed

title: SPA renders stale logged-in dashboard on 401 (/me) instead of redirecting to /login
description: UX bug observed during 002 dogfooding (not in spec-002 tasks). When the backend rejects a cached access token (e.g. after a dev JWT keyring regen on restart), the /me call returns 401 but the SPA keeps rendering a stale 'Signed in as…' dashboard rather than redirecting to /login. Repro: cache a token, restart backend (new keyring), reload SPA. Expected: a 401 on /me clears the session and routes to /login. Workaround today: click Sign out. Likely in the web/ auth interceptor/guard. Non-blocking; surfaced in the 002 HANDOFF across multiple sessions.
close_reason: NOT REPRODUCING. Verified in real browser: expired-access+valid-refresh transparently refreshes (401 → /auth/refresh 200 → retry 200); invalid-refresh and no-token both redirect to /login; no stale dashboard. Token iat==page-load proved the 'zombie' read was benign console noise. Evidence: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-02-support-desk-usability-design.md. No code fix was needed.

title: US4 follow-up: inbox-settings single saving() signal serializes mutations
description: web/src/app/pages/support/inbox-settings.ts uses one saving() signal, so a slow per-domain Verify disables the add-domain and add-address forms too. Per-action loading signals (verify already has verifyingId) would let independent actions proceed. UX rough edge, non-blocking.
close_reason: Closed

title: US4 follow-up: RSA-2048 DKIM fallback (Ed25519-only shipped)
description: T057 shipped Ed25519-only DKIM signing. research R3 specifies RSA-2048 as a fallback for receivers that don't validate Ed25519 DKIM. Add per-domain RSA-2048 keygen + signing as a fallback/secondary selector. Non-blocking; Ed25519 is widely supported and go-msgauth signs+verifies it.
design:
SCOPED 2026-06-02 (deferred to feature work, NOT polish — ~MEDIUM, multi-day).

GOAL: 'RSA-2048 fallback for receivers that don't validate Ed25519 DKIM' (research R3). The ONLY design that achieves this is DUAL-PUBLISH + DUAL-SIGN (not pick-one-per-domain): generate BOTH keys, publish TWO DNS TXT records (k=ed25519 + k=rsa), emit TWO DKIM-Signature headers so each receiver validates whichever it supports.

CHANGE MAP (file:line):
1. Keygen — internal/ticketing/identity.go:~190 CreateEmailDomain() ed25519.GenerateKey only → also rsa.GenerateKey(2048); seal both.
2. Schema — migrations/ NEW migration: add dkim_rsa_selector, dkim_rsa_public_key, dkim_rsa_private_key_ref to email_domain (+ db/schema.sql + make generate for dbgen). get_send_identity DEFINER (migration 0023) must also return the RSA columns.
3. Send path — internal/platform/notify/sender_subscriber.go:189-228 selectCustomIdentity(): line 216 len(keyBytes)!=ed25519.PrivateKeySize HARD-REJECTS non-ed25519 — must load+unseal BOTH keys; smtp.go signDKIM already crypto.Signer-agnostic (no change) but Send must sign twice (two DKIM-Signature headers) OR Mail carries []*DKIMConfig.
4. DNS publication — internal/ticketing/identity.go:~522 toEmailDomain() hardcodes 'k=ed25519' → expose a SECOND DKIMRecord for the RSA selector. API contract change.
5. OpenAPI — contracts/openapi.yaml DNSChallenge: dkim_record (single) → dkim_records (list) or add dkim_rsa_record.
6. Tests — internal/platform/notify/dkim_signing_integration_test.go (DB+infra): add RSA keygen+sign+verify; assert BOTH signatures present + cryptographically valid. Plus identity keygen test.

NOTE: system DKIM (cmd/manyforge/main.go dkimConfigFromCfg) ALREADY parses both PKCS8-ed25519 and PKCS1-RSA — custom keygen should mirror. Ed25519 is widely supported so this is genuinely non-blocking.
notes: Deferred to feature work (decision 2026-06-02). Backlog-clear pass closed the other 6 spec-002 follow-ups (9li/0yv/zzr/no9/7c0/mu7); 3jt is feature-scale (dual-publish DKIM), see design field.

title: Authenticated JSON API lacks request-body-size cap (httpx.DecodeJSON)
description: Final US3 review (T044-T051) flagged: httpx.DecodeJSON streams the request body with no http.MaxBytesReader, and the /api/v1 protected routes have no global request-size middleware (only ReadHeaderTimeout is set; InboundMaxBytes covers only webhook/SMTP). Pre-existing across US1/US2 reply/note and now US3 triage. Authenticated + permission-gated so practical DoS risk is low, but constitution input-validation/defense-in-depth calls for a per-helper or middleware body cap. Add http.MaxBytesReader in DecodeJSON (or a body-limit middleware on protected routes). NOT a US3 regression. Relates to epic manyforge-n0q.
close_reason: Closed

title: US2 follow-up: outbound body_html dropped at MIME layer (text/plain only)
description: body_html is plumbed end-to-end (DB -> get_send_context -> notify.Mail.BodyHTML) but buildMIME (internal/platform/notify/smtp.go) only emits text/plain + BodyText. Harmless in US2 (composer is text-only, body_html always nil) but a latent gap: an API caller can POST body_html, see it stored/returned, yet it's never delivered. Add a multipart/alternative sender (text + html) in a follow-up. Surfaced in US2 final review.
close_reason: Closed

title: US2 doc accuracy: OpenAPI/Mail comments overstate sending identity + [#ref] subject
description: The /reply OpenAPI summary says 'from the verified custom identity if present' and notify.Mail.Subject comment says it 'carries the [#<reply_token>] tag', but US2 sends from the system identity only (get_send_context joins kind='system') and the subject is plain 'Re: ...' (the [#ref] subject-token fallback is NOT built; threading rides the VERP Reply-To). Tighten the docs/comments or mark deferred. Custom-identity/DKIM = US4; [#ref] subject matching = deferred. Surfaced in US2 final review.
close_reason: Closed

title: US1 test-hardening follow-ups (non-blocking)
description:
Minor test/quality items found during US1 review (all units APPROVED; these strengthen pins/coverage). Batch before final US1 review:
1. replytoken: add deterministic TestVerifyReplyTokenNonCanonicalEncoding (fixed key+uuid, padding-bit flips on BOTH id and sig segments) — current flipped-last-byte case only exercises the canonical-base64 malleability defense ~6.25% of runs (threading_idempotency_test.go:46).
2. SMTP: add multi-RCPT fan-out test (two accepted recipients in one DATA → assert two ingests w/ correct per-recipient EnvelopeRecipient); smtp.go:253 loop currently untested for fan-out.
3. webhook: add a test for the sha256= prefix-strip path in verify() (webhook.go:77) — currently dead/untested; or remove the tolerance (YAGNI, contract is bare hex).
4. webhook: clarifying comment on remoteIP() (X-Forwarded-For intentionally ignored — RemoteAddr only, not trusted for routing/auth).
5. smtp_integration_test port reservation: one-line comment that close-then-rebind is inherently racy (test-only).
Found across f2339ec/0eff959/f37d776.
close_reason: Closed

See docs/ROADMAP.md. Size: S-M.

title: US3 nit: validate agent_run target_type + document target_id as hint-not-gate
description: Holistic review (US3 close-out): agent_run.target_type is free-text (no CHECK) while trigger has a CHECK — add a CHECK or service-layer allowlist {'ticket'} for consistency (blast radius is nil today: parameterized SQL + string-compare only). Also add a one-line comment at RunService.Trigger noting target_id is a prompt HINT, not an ownership gate (tools are the authorization boundary under the agent's RLS) so a future reader doesn't mistake it for a missed ownership check.

title: US3 follow-up: share permission-key constants between tool registry, ticketing handlers, and migrations
description: internal/agents/tools.go hardcodes RequiredPerm string literals (tickets.read/write/assign/reply); internal/ticketing/handler.go and the 0015 migration use the same bare strings. If the permission catalog ever defines exported Perm* constants, these call sites should reference them so a rename fails at compile time rather than silently mis-gating. Low risk (perms are migration-seeded). Surfaced in US3 Task 4 code-quality review (M2).

title: knownProviders set should track the ai_provider PG enum
description: internal/agents/credential.go knownProviders is an untyped map[string]bool that must stay in lockstep with the ai_provider Postgres enum (migration 0025) + dbgen.AiProvider* constants. Convert to dbgen.AiProvider* constants or a generated set when the enum next changes (e.g. adding a provider). Flagged in US1a final review; non-blocking. Part of Spec 003 (manyforge-deo).

title: US4 follow-up: DKIM master-key base64/hex parse disambiguation
description: internal/platform/config envKey32 prefers the decode yielding exactly 32 bytes; a 64-char all-hex-alphabet base64 string is taken as a 32-byte hex key. Cannot arise from a correctly-encoded 32-byte key (44-char base64 or 64-char hex are unambiguous). Optional hardening: require explicit base64:/hex: prefix or anchor hex on ^[0-9a-fA-F]{64}$. Nit.
close_reason: Closed

value: Lesson (manyforge UI): URL-driven Playwright e2e navigate straight to a page URL, so they PASS even when nothing in the app links to that page. US4's inbox-settings shipped reachable only by typing the URL — dashboard had no Support link (since US1) and ticket-list had no Inbox-settings link. Always verify a new page is reachable via real clicks; pin nav hops with e2e (data-testids nav-support, inbox-settings-link).

Query-count regression tests via pgx QueryTracer (db.WithTracer + testdb.AppDSN): pgxpool's AfterRelease health-check (SELECT current_setting) runs LAZILY and lands in the NEXT call's trace window, so a naive 'total queries equal' assertion is flaky. Count only queries touching the domain table (filter SQL contains 'ticket'), ignoring begin/commit/set_config/current_setting plumbing — deterministic. See internal/ticketing/read_n1_integration_test.go (manyforge-iiq).

sqlc.embed + folded aggregates: COALESCE((SELECT array_agg(x) ...), '{}') infers Go type interface{}; move the cast OUTSIDE so the column is unambiguously text[] -> []string: COALESCE((...), '{}')::text[] AS tags. Also: changing a plpgsql function's RETURN TABLE columns or args needs DROP FUNCTION + CREATE in the migration (CREATE OR REPLACE cannot alter return type/signature) — keep a down migration restoring the prior signature. See migrations/0024 (manyforge-axq) + db/query/ticketing.sql.

After a subagent runs 'make generate' (sqlc), gopls/IDE often shows PHANTOM compile errors like 'dbgen.Ticket undefined' / 'q.GetTicket undefined' across internal/ticketing — a mid-regeneration + stale build-tag transient, NOT real. Always verify with 'go build ./...' (committed tree builds clean). Recurred ~4x during US3 (spec 002). Also: //go:build integration|contract test files always show a benign gopls 'No packages found … build tags' warning.

Dev live stack (manyforge): backend via air on :8081 — `set -a; . ./.air.env; set +a; air` (.air.env gitignored: DB url + MANYFORGE_DKIM_MASTER_KEY); frontend `cd web && npx ng serve --port 4300 --proxy-config proxy.conf.json` (proxy /api -> :8081). Dev Postgres localhost:55432 (superuser manyforge:devpassword, app manyforge_app:devpassword). Login live-demo@manyforge.test / DevPassw0rd! (owner of Acme Holdings 7bbeb32e-7c98-4c8f-966b-70acdb440dce). Migrate: migrate -path migrations -database pgx5://manyforge:devpassword@localhost:55432/manyforge?sslmode=disable up.

When presenting options/choices to Josh, ALWAYS number them (1., 2., 3.) — never bullet-point lists. He corrected this twice. Prefer the AskUserQuestion selectable tool for decisions; if listing choices in prose, use a numbered list.
